caption: This is the Project Detail page for UHUS. Every dataset is reached from here, all measured at the neighborhood (dong) level across Seoul's 422 neighborhoods.

The daily weather data records each neighborhood's max temperature, precipitation, humidity, and apparent temperature for 2024. It defines hot days (apparent temp >= 33C) and mild days (18-26C, no rain, no holiday).
Because weather is temporal, the 3D map animates over time. Play the sequence to watch the temperature field rise and fall across the year, peaking in mid-summer.
The sales data records daily card-transaction amounts per neighborhood, the behavioral signal behind retail heat sensitivity. A retail total sums the 19 core retail sectors.
The 85 industries are grouped into 6 themes, each drawn as one ring per neighborhood, pulsing day by day.
Differences can also be shown by choosing a different form: columns, a flat choropleth, or a dominant-group view.
The Urban Features dataset describes each neighborhood's context (demographics, accessibility, land use, built environment) across 21 variables that explain why heat sensitivity differs.
Shown as a choropleth, each urban feature can be compared directly against the heat-sensitivity index.
Hovering over a neighborhood shows its name and details: the mapped value, its heat-sensitivity index, and its citywide rank.
The SHAP dataset explains the model: for each neighborhood, how much every input pushed the predicted index up or down.
It decomposes the index into contributing factors, ranking which urban features drive each neighborhood's result. It explains model behavior, not causation.
The RHSI, the Retail Heat-Sensitivity Index, is the study's main output: the log-ratio of average retail sales on hot days versus mild days, per neighborhood.
A negative value means sales fall on hot days, so that neighborhood is heat-sensitive. The map colors each neighborhood by this index.
You can choose a target area, the whole of Seoul, a district (gu), or a neighborhood (dong), by clicking directly on the map or through the spatial selection boxes.
The map panel also offers other options, such as a flat 2D view or automatic rotation.
